scope_note: QA Langlands GL4 Tensor Product (Rankin-Selberg Local Factor) cert [405]. Primary sources: Jacquet, H., Piatetski-Shapiro, I.I., Shalika, J.A. (1983) 'Rankin-Selberg Convolutions' American Journal of Mathematics 105 pp.367-464 doi:10.2307/2374264; Shahidi, F. (1981) 'On certain L-functions' American Journal of Mathematics 103 pp.297-355 doi:10.2307/2374219; Bump, D. (1997) 'Automorphic Forms and Representations' Cambridge ISBN 978-0-521-65818-1 (Ch.2.6 tensor product L-factors). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): For f=2.2.5.1-125.1-a (GL_2/Q(sqrt5), CM by Q(zeta_5)) with split prime p≡1(mod5) and eigenvalue a_p=(u,v) in Z[phi], the tensor product of the two GL_2 local components pi_p and pi_{pbar} gives a degree-4 GL_4 Euler polynomial: R_p(Y) = 1 - N*Y + p*(T^2-2*N-2*p)*Y^2 - p^2*N*Y^3 + p^4*Y^4 where T=Tr_{Q(sqrt5)/Q}(a_p)=2u+v and N=N_{Q(sqrt5)/Q}(a_p)=u^2+u*v-v^2. Three checks: (C1) integer coefficients: all four non-trivial coefficients (-N, p(T^2-2N-2p), -p^2*N, p^4) lie in Z for all 22 split primes p<=500; (C2) weight-2 palindrome: R_p(Y)=p^4*Y^4*R_p(1/(p^2*Y)), equivalently a_3=p^2*a_1 and a_4=p^4*a_0; (C3) algebraic derivation: R_p equals the product Q_2(alpha_1*Y)*Q_2(alpha_2*Y) where alpha_1,alpha_2 are Satake params of Q_1=1-embed1(a_p)*alpha+p*alpha^2; by Vieta alpha_1*alpha_2=p and alpha_1+alpha_2=embed1(a_p), so the product of all four cross factors (1-alpha_i*gamma_j*Y) collapses to R_p via a_1*a_2=N and a_1^2+a_2^2=T^2-2N (all integer). Langlands ladder: [403]->[404] GL4 induction ->[405] THIS tensor-product local factor. Theorem NT: embed1 and embed2 are observer projections only; all tensor-product coefficient computation is in Z. Derived 2026-06-13.
